title: Namespaces, Metadata, Labels, Selectors, Ownership, and Deletion · Cluster Case: K8s Annotations Managed Metadata
summary: Several teams share a cluster where a selector overlaps another release, an owner is recreated, and a finalizer leaves a namespace terminating. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s annotations managed metadata increment in resource identity and lifecycle policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate identifying labels from non-identifying annotations and preserve controller, tooling, audit, and rollout metadata ownership.
Design stable recommended labels and equality or set selectors without accidental overlap, mutable identity, or controller mismatch.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example namespace contains similarly named objects, copied labels, a dangling owner reference, and a finalizer blocking deletion. Design names, labels, selectors, annotations, ownership, garbage collection, quota boundaries, and deletion evidence. Competency probe: Separate identifying labels from non-identifying annotations and preserve controller, tooling, audit, and rollout metadata ownership. A defensible solution applies K8s Annotations Managed Metadata, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to resource identity and lifecycle policy.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-annotations-managed-metadata — Separate identifying labels from non-identifying annotations and preserve controller, tooling, audit, and rollout metadata ownership.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-metadata-namespaces-ownership-5
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-metadata-namespaces-ownership-5
    artifact: metadata-policy.yaml
    decision: worked k8s metadata namespaces ownership 5
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      namespace: intake
      labels: app.kubernetes.io/name=status-api
      selector-owner: deployment-status-api
      owner-uid: retained
      deletion: foreground
      finalizer-controller: archive-cleanup
    verify: kubectl get all -n intake --show-labels -o json && kubectl get namespace intake -o json
    failure: Reject namespace-as-sandbox claims, overlapping selectors, name-only owner matching, or blind finalizer removal.
    repair: Separate scope and security, stabilize identity labels, trace owner UIDs and dependents, and identify external cleanup before deletion.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.text: Large arbitrary application data belongs in labels because selectors can ignore it. This fails the K8s Annotations Managed Metadata contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
